mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution of Kubernetes that provides a comprehensive platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework for enhanced integration and usability. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the powerful features and enterprise readiness it offers.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is necessary for managing Tanzu Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

```

> This command downloads and extracts the Tanzu CLI to /usr/local/bin.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> This command initializes the Tanzu CLI and prepares it for use.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

```

> This command creates a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes in the 'us-west-2' region.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to manage the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> This command retrieves the kubeconfig for the 'my-cluster' and sets the context.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster to check their status.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> This command deletes the 'my-cluster' and all associated resources.

uninstall.title: Remove Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> This command removes the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster data. This step upgrades the Kubernetes version of the cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

```

> This command upgrades 'my-cluster' to Kubernetes version 1.29.1.

upgrade.title: Verify upgrade
upgrade.description:
This step checks that the upgrade was successful.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes to verify their status after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Post-upgrade checks
upgrade.description:
Ensure all workloads are running correctly after the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> This command checks the status of all pods across namespaces.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified context does not exist in your kubeconfig.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl config get-contexts' to list available contexts and ensure you are using the correct one.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud provider's resource availability and adjust the node count or region accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service on the node has failed to start.

**Fix:**
SSH into the node and check the kubelet logs using 'journalctl -u kubelet'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Verify your network settings and ensure the API server is running. Use 'kubectl cluster-info' to check the server status.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI commands for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older Kubernetes versions (1.26 and below).
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to update your CLI to the latest version and review the new features.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
